headline: TurboQuant Panic: Why Market Is Wrong About Google's Newest AI Breakthrough
summary: Alphabet Inc. Google rattled global memory stocks after unveiling its TurboQuant AI algorithm, triggering a sharp sell-off amid fears that improved efficiency could dampen demand for memory chips. However, analysts pushed back on the bearish reaction, arguing the technology is more likely to expand AI use cases and ultimately drive higher long-term demand—framing the pullback as a potential "buy the dip" opportunity for investors. headline: Micron Eyes Japan Display Plant As Capacity Plans Meet Valuation Questions
summary: Micron Technology (NasdaqGS:MU) is reportedly in talks to acquire a Japan Display LCD plant in Japan. The potential deal would add assembly and testing capacity for advanced chips beyond Micron's existing memory operations. The move would represent an expansion of Micron's manufacturing footprint in a key Asian production region. Micron Technology comes into this reported deal after a very large 3 year share price gain and a strong move over the past year, with the stock up 305.2% across 12...
